Baku as we explore this UNESCO world heritage site we'll visit iconic landmarks such as the maiden Tower and of the Palace of the shervances you'll feel like you've stepped back in time as you soak up the ancient history and architecture of this magical place number two flame Towers Highland Park funicular Railway towards flame Tower Martyrs lay next up we'll take a thrilling ride up the funicular Railway towards the flame Towers one of the most iconic landmarks in Baku from the top of the towers you'll be treated to stunning panoramic views of the city and the Caspian Sea we'll also visit the Highland Park where you can take in breathtaking views of the city and the martyrs Lane a peaceful and poignant Memorial to those who lost their lives during the nagorno-karabakh conflict number three nizami Street after that will hit the bustling streets of nazami the main shopping district in Baku from trendy boutiques to traditional markets this vibrant area has something for everyone you'll be swept up in the energy and excitement of this bustling District as we explore the shops cafes and restaurants number four Baku Boulevard national flag Square Baku Crystal Hall no visit to Baku is complete without a trip to the Baku Boulevard a stunning Promenade that stretches along the Caspian Sea we'll take a leisurely stroll along the boardwalk stopping to admire the iconic national flag square and the stunning Baku Crystal Hall a world-class venue for concerts and events number five carpet Museum mini Venice Baku Denise Mall Baku Baku I for our next stop we'll head to the carpet Museum a unique and fascinating Museum dedicated to the art of carpet weaving we'll also visit the Charming mini Venice Baku a picturesque Recreation of Venice complete with canals and gondolas after that we'll hit the Denise Mall Baku a shopping and entertainment complex [Music] where you can take a ride on the Baku eye a giant Ferris wheel that offers stunning views of the city number six Hadar aliev Center our next stop is the Hadar aliev Center an architectural Marvel that showcases the best of modern design from the stunning curved lines of the building to the interactive exhibits inside this is a must visit destination for anyone interested in contemporary art and architecture number seven attesh God Temple will continue our tour with a visit to the Atta Scott Temple a fascinating and historic site located just outside of Baku this ancient Fire temple was once a place of worship for zoroastrians and Hindus and it's a fascinating insight into the cultural and religious history of Azerbaijan number eight yanardog next up we'll head to yanardog a natural gas fire that has been burning for centuries this is a unique and Unforgettable sight that you won't want to miss as we history and culture of azerbaijan's ancient inhabitants number 10. mud volcanoes last but not least we'll end our tour with a visit to the mud volcanoes a unique and otherworldly landscape that is hope you enjoyed our virtual tour of the top 10 places to visit in Baku Azerbaijan forget to subscribe to our incredible Azerbaijan YouTube channel for More Travel inspiration and Adventures thanks videos.title: അസർബൈജാനിൽ കണ്ടിരിക്കേണ്ട സ്ഥലങ്ങൾ | Best Places to Visit in Azerbaijan | Baku, Gabala & Yanar Dag
videos.description:
Welcome to Azerbaijan – the Land of Fire! In this travel video, join me as I explore the most beautiful and must-visit destinations across Azerbaijan, including Baku, Gabala, Yanar Dag Fire Mountain, Ateshgah Fire Temple, and the Seven Beauties Waterfall.
Our journey begins at Yanar Dag, a mysterious mountain that burns endlessly day and night, even in the rain. From there, we visit the Fire Temple (Ateshgah), a historic site once used by fire-worshippers, followed by a stop at the world’s first petroleum field—a reminder of Azerbaijan’s rich natural gas heritage.
Next, we travel to Gabala, surrounded by snow-capped mountains and lush forests. We enjoy the breathtaking Tufandag Mountain cable car ride, explore the tranquil Nohur Lake, and witness the magical Seven Beauties Waterfall (Yeddi Gozal) hidden deep inside the forest. Gabala is a paradise for nature lovers and adventure seekers alike.
Finally, we reach Baku, the vibrant capital city of Azerbaijan. From the panoramic views of Highland Park and the iconic Flame Towers, to the modern Caspian Waterfront Mall, Baku Eye, Carpet Museum, and the romantic Little Venice, every corner of Baku blends history and modernity beautifully.
We also explore the ancient Icherisheher (Old City)—a UNESCO World Heritage Site filled with narrow streets, stone towers, and historical wonders like the Maiden Tower and Palace of the Shirvanshahs. The journey ends at the lively Nizami Street, one of Baku’s most happening spots filled with restaurants, shops, and local culture.
If you’re planning a trip to Azerbaijan, this video will give you a complete travel guide covering the top attractions, unique experiences, and local food highlights including Plov, Kebabs, and the famous Chorek bread.

📍 Places Covered:
Yanar Dag (Burning Mountain)
Ateshgah Fire Temple
Petroleum Fields of Azerbaijan
Gabala & Nohur Lake
Tufandag Mountain & Cable Car Ride
Seven Beauties Waterfall (Yeddi Gozal)
Baku City Tour
Highland Park & Flame Towers
Caspian Waterfront Mall
Carpet Museum & Little Venice
Baku Boulevard & Funicular Ride
Heydar Aliyev Center
Icherisheher (Old City)
Maiden Tower & Shirvanshah Palace
Nizami Street

🎥 Watch till the end for a complete experience of this fascinating country — from fire mountains to snowy peaks, from ancient history to modern architecture!
🇦🇿 Azerbaijan truly is a land where fire meets beauty.

📩 Tour arranged by: Eurasian Voyage (Ashiq – Malayali Tour Operator in Azerbaijan)
👉 Contact: +994508706475 (WhatsApp / Call)

#azerbaijan #baku #travel #gabala

videos.title: Top 10 things to do in Baku, Azerbaijan | Travel Guide
videos.description:
Looking for the best places to visit in Baku? Discover the perfect mix of ancient heritage, modern wonders, and rich culture in Azerbaijan’s vibrant capital! In this travel guide, we’ll walk you through the top 10 things to do in Baku, featuring iconic landmarks, hidden gems, and unforgettable experiences you simply can’t miss.

🌍 From wandering the medieval streets of Icherisheher (Old City) to marveling at futuristic architecture like the Heydar Aliyev Center, Baku offers something for every kind of traveler. Visit the Shirvanshah Palace, Maiden Tower, and the quirky Miniature Book Museum, or shop, dine, and stroll along the lively Nizami Street and Seaside Boulevard.

📸 For the best city views, head up to Highland Park, take a ride on the Baku Funicular, or enjoy sunset magic on the Baku Eye Ferris wheel. Don't miss the dazzling Flame Towers lighting up the night sky, and be sure to explore the Azerbaijan Carpet Museum and peaceful canals of Little Venice.

🍽️ Hungry after all the sightseeing? Treat yourself to local favorites like Shah Pilaf, Chorek bread, and juicy Azerbaijani kebabs—flavors that reflect the heart of this beautiful land.

Whether you’re a history buff, architecture enthusiast, foodie, or just a curious traveler, these are truly the best things to do in Baku. Pack your bags and get ready to explore a city where East meets West in the most magical way.

🕒 Timestamps:
00:00 – Welcome to Baku, Azerbaijan
00:42 – Icherisheher (Old City)
01:16 – Shirvanshah Palace
01:52 – Miniature Book Museum
02:32 – Maiden Tower

07:35 – Azerbaijani Cuisine Tips
07:52 – Final Thoughts & Subscribe

Watch the full video now and start planning your Baku adventure!
Don’t forget to like, share, and subscribe for more travel guides from around the world.

what to do in baku azerbaijan
places to visit in baku azerbaijan
places to visit in baku city
things to do in baku city
videos.transcript: [Music] Welcome to Baku, Azerbaijan's charming capital. A city where the east and west seamlessly blend, creating a vibrant and captivating atmosphere. Nestled along the shores of the Caspian Sea, Baku uniquely sits 28 m below sea level, making it the lowest lying national capital globally. Renowned as the city of winds, Baku offers diverse experiences from ancient palaces and intriguing museums to contemporary architecture and delicious cuisine. In this video, let's explore the top 10 things to do in Baku, highlighting the best places you absolutely can't miss. Start your journey in the enchanting old city, also known as Icher Shaker. Surrounded by ancient fortress walls, this UNESCO World Heritage site is the historical heart of Baku. Stroll through its narrow, winding alleys, explore charming shops, cozy cafes, and historical landmarks including Shervancha Palace, Maiden Tower, and the Miniature Book Museum. Staying in this area offers you convenient access to many of Baku's highlights. [Music] One of Shahher's gems is the majestic Shervin Sha Palace known as the pearl of Azerbaijani architecture. Built in the 15th century during the reign of the Shervin Sha dynasty, this palace complex beautifully combines Persian, Islamic and Caucasian styles. Wander through its royal chambers, visit the ancient mosque and discover historical artifacts displayed in its museum. The palace opens daily from 10:00 a.m. to 6:00 p.m. and the entry fee is around 15 Azerbaijani Manat. Just next door, step into the world's only museum of miniature books. This fascinating collection showcases over 6,500 tiny books from 65 different countries. Marvel at miniature editions of the Quran, Bible, and Mahabarata, with the smallest book measuring just 0.75 millime. Established in 2002, the museum opens daily except on Mondays and Thursdays from 11:00 a.m. to 5:00 p.m. Entry is free, making this a delightful stop during your visit. [Music] The iconic maiden Tower is another must-see landmark within a Kerishah. Rising 97 ft above the city, this 12th century structure offers stunning panoramic views of Baku. While its exact purpose remains a mystery with theories ranging from defensive fortress to ancient temple, local legend narrates the tragic tale of a princess who jumped from the tower to avoid an unwanted marriage, hence its name. The tower opens daily from 10:00 a.m. to 6:00 p.m. with an entry fee of about 15 minutes. Now, let's head to Nami Street, Baku's vibrant commercial and social hub. Named after the legendary poet Nami Gunjavi, this bustling street brim with trendy boutiques, international brands, and inviting restaurants. Fully pedestrianized, the street is adorned with twinkling lights and lively music, offering a delightful atmosphere throughout the day and night. making it ideal for shopping, dining, and enjoying the vibrant local culture. For an unforgettable view of Baku's skyline, visit Highland Park, also known as Upland Park. Located on a hilltop, this park offers spectacular views of landmarks like the Flame Towers, Seaside Boulevard, and the Caspian Sea. A peaceful escape from the bustling city below, Highland Park is ideal for sunset views and memorable photographs. Descending from Highland Park, you'll reach Seaside Boulevard, a picturesque prominade stretching nearly 4 km along the Caspian Sea. Established in 1909, it's perfect for leisurely walks or relaxing by the sea. Enjoy numerous attractions here, including charming cafes, beautiful gardens, and entertainment centers. Next door is Denise Mall, an architectural marvel resembling a lotus flower. Opened in 2020, Denise Mall houses over 70 premium shops, extensive dining areas, and entertainment venues. Designed by Chapman Taylor, this shopping center blends luxury shopping and leisure seamlessly, offering visitors a world-class retail experience. Just a short walk away, don't miss the iconic Baku Eye, a giant ferris wheel standing 60 m tall, offering breathtaking panoramic views of Baku's cityscape and the Caspian Sea. It's an especially magical experience at sunset. The ferris wheel operates daily from 10:00 a.m. to 11:00 p.m. providing a romantic vantage point to capture memorable photos. Dive into Azerbaijan's rich textile history at the Azerbaijan National Carpet Museum. Uniquely designed to resemble a rolled carpet, home to over 10,000 artifacts, including carpets, ceramics, metal work, and jewelry. This museum beautifully showcases Azerbaijani heritage. It opens daily except Mondays from 10:00 a.m. to 6:00 p.m. with tickets priced around 10 Monet. Nearby is Little Venice, a charming attraction reminiscent of Italy's romantic waterways. This artificial canal network features gondola style boats, charming bridges, and lovely surroundings, making it a fantastic spot for a short, relaxing boat ride lasting around 10 minutes. [Music] For a quick and scenic trip, hop on the Baku finicular connecting Seaside Boulevard in Highland Park. Operating since 1960, this historic railway offers impressive city views as you glide up the hillside. It runs daily except Mondays from 10:00 a.m. to 8:00 p.m. And the ticket price is just one minute. High above the city, the striking flame towers illuminate Baku's night sky with spectacular LED displays symbolizing Azerbaijan's ancient tradition of fire worship. These towers house luxury residences, offices, and hotels. Viewing these iconic buildings, particularly at night, is an unforgettable site. [Music] Finally, immerse yourself in contemporary architecture at the Hadar Ali Center. This futuristic structure designed by the acclaimed architect Zaha Hadid includes a museum, conference halls, and exhibition spaces. Don't miss its unique classic car collection featuring vintage vehicles from around the globe. The center opens daily from 11:00 a.m. to 6:00 p.m. with entry fees varying based on exhibitions. When in Baku, don't forget to sample authentic Azerbaijani cuisine. Sha pilaf, a delicious rice dish baked inside crispy lavish bread, is a national favorite. Pair it with freshly baked chore bread and succulent kebabs for a truly mouthwatering experience. If you enjoyed this video, please subscribe and help support my channel's growth.

safetyNotes: Don't miss the dazzling Flame Towers lighting up the night sky, and be sure to explore the Azerbaijan Carpet Museum and peaceful canals of Little Venice

videos.description:
#azerbaijan #bakunightlife #baku 

Book all your Azerbaijan day trips from india for cheap  here: https://www.klook.com/

USE MY CODE: CHALOLETSGO TO GET 10% DISCOUNT ON FIRST TIME BOOKING ON KLOOK WEBSITE OR APP. 

Don't Make These Mistakes in Azerbaijan + Baku Nightlife Scam! Azerbaijan Tour Guide

baku, Azerbaijan, is a vibrant city with a lot to offer tourists. However, like any major tourist destination, it's important to be aware of common scams that can target unsuspecting travelers.

in this video, we'll discuss some of the most frequent scams encountered by visitors to Baku, including:

Taxi Scams:

Refusal to use the meter: Insist on using the meter or negotiate the fare beforehand.
Detours: Be vigilant and use navigation apps to ensure the driver is taking the most direct route.
Fake taxi drivers: Only use official taxis or ride-sharing services.
Currency Exchange Scams:

Wrong billing scams: double check amount before paying by credit cards.

Misleading menus: Carefully review the menu and prices before ordering.
Extra charges: Check the bill carefully for any unexpected charges.
Pickpocketing and Theft:

Be mindful of your belongings: Keep valuables secure in a money belt or a secure pocket.
Stay vigilant in crowded areas: Be aware of your surroundings and avoid displaying valuables openly.

Tour Scams:

Book tours through reputable agencies: Avoid booking tours from street vendors or touts.
Read reviews before booking: Check online reviews to ensure you're booking with a legitimate company.

This video will provide you with valuable tips and advice on how to avoid these scams and have a safe and enjoyable trip to Baku. We'll also share some general safety tips for traveling in Azerbaijan.

Remember:

Trust your instincts.
Be cautious of overly friendly strangers.
By being informed and prepared, you can minimize the risk of falling victim to scams and have a wonderful experience in Baku.

#TravelTips #TravelSafety #Scams #TravelAdvice #BakuTravel #AzerbaijanTravel #TouristTips #staysafe

videos.transcript: In today's video, I will tell you the top 15 places of Baku, the capital of Azerbaijan. If you are planning to visit Azerbaijan, then this video will help you a lot. Azerbaijan is a beautiful country in Central Asia, which used to be a part of the USSR. The capital of Azerbaijan is Baku, but the Azerbaijani people call it Baaki. If you go to visit Baku, it will not seem less than any European city because its architecture is very high. It is beautiful, I did a three day trip to Azerbaijan from Dubai. If you are planning to visit Azerbaijan, then after watching my videos, you will not need to watch any video. I will discuss everything in detail. In the upcoming videos, I will tell you what activities you have to do by going to Azerbaijan, what things you have to take care of, you need to avoid scam too much and whatever is the important point, I will discuss in the series of Azerbaijan. Then let's discuss the top 15 places of Baku in this video. Number one Nizami Street, Nizami Street is named after Persian Poet Nizami Ganjavi. You must have heard the story of Laila Majnu. The story of Laila Majnu was written by Nizami Ganjavi. Nizami Street is most famous in Baku because the architecture here is very beautiful. Photos and videos look good here. Restaurants and cafes are also built. You will also get Indian food here. If you are planning to visit Baku, then book your hotel around Nizami Street because there is a lot to see in 2-3 km around Nizami Street. Nizami Street has one of the best restaurants and cafes as well as clubs, but if you are planning to go to the club, then you need to be very careful about fraud. Number two Baku Boulevard is very close to Nizam Street, Baku. you have to cross the main road. As soon as you cross the road, you will come to Baku Boulevard. It is built on the banks of the Caspian Sea. Here you will see people walking, running and playing. While walking through Baku Boulevard, you will explore many things which I am going to tell further. Number three Carpet Museum: Carpets are very famous in Azerbaijan, so there is a carpet museum built here. This museum has the world's largest carpet collection. In this museum, you will get complete information related to carpet. The building of Carpet Museum looks very beautiful because its shape is folded rug. This museum is built very close to Baku Boulevard. The timing of the museum is *Tuesday to Friday 10 am to 6 pm, Saturday Sunday 11 am to 7 pm. Ticket price is AZN 10 in Indian currency INR 510 and on Monday It remains closed. Number four funicular its a railway track of 455 meters. If you go up while riding the funicular, you will get a very good view. I went in the evening, so I had to wait a little because there was a long line. It has two stations, one is the lower station, one is the upper station, the lower station is near the carpet museum and the upper station is in Highland Park. One-way ticket price of the funicular is only 1 AZN . The timing of the month is 10 AM to 8 PM and is closed on Mondays. Number five Highland Park Highland Park is one of the most popular and interesting attractions of Baku. Near the Baku Boulevard there is the Carpet Museum. There is a funicular station near the Carpet Museum. You have to go to Highland Park only through the funicular. You will have a very good experience. Highland Park is built at a very high altitude, so from here you can go to the Old Town. You can get a very good view of Baku, Deniz Mall, Baku Boulevard, you will get a very good view of all this and the photos and videos will also be very good and from here you can also get a very good view of the Flame Tower, so you can also take good photos and videos. Flame Towers are most famous in Baku. Azerbaijan means Land of Fire, so these towers have been built inspired by flame. One has an office and the other is residential. And in the third place there is Fairmont Hotel and at night there is a light show on the flame towers which looks very beautiful. Number Six Deniz Mall. This mall is built on the banks of Caspian sea. Its architecture is very beautiful. You can do shopping from this mall and can also do sliding. There are two types of sliding in this mall, one is located on the fifth floor and one is located on the third floor and there is a very good view around Deniz Mall too. So you can also take good photos and videos. Number Seven Little Venice Little Venice is located in Baku Boulevard. You can walk from Nizam Street and reach Little Venice. You can enjoy the boat in Little Venice. There is also a guide along with three persons in it. There are also restaurants in Little Venice. You can also go near the restaurants and take your good photos and videos. In Little Venice, the per person ticket price of boat Ride is 5 AZN and timing is 11 am to 9 pm. Number eight Maiden Tower is a UNESCO World Heritage Site. This tower was built in the 12th century. When viewed from above, the Maidan Tower looks like the number nine. Below this tower, a map of Old Baku is made of metal. The entry ticket of the Maidan Tower is 15 AZN and the timing is 10 am to 6 pm. Number Nine Old Baku is a UNESCO World Heritage Site. Old Baku is also called the Walled City of Baku because there is a wall around it. Old Baku encompasses the history of Azerbaijan. It has a very old history . Like Zoroastrian, Sasanian, Arabic, Persian, Shirvani, Ottoman, and Russian, all of them have ruled here. The walls of Old Baku were built in the 12th century. The oldest restaurant is built in Old Baku. The oldest mosque is Juma Mosque. Here you can also buy souvenir items. I have made a separate vlog of Old Baku. If you look at that vlog, you will get a lot of idea of ​​Old Baku. Number ten Heydar Aliyev Cultural Center Heydar Aliyev was the third President of Azerbaijan and he was a very famous politician. The architecture of this building has been made very beautiful. You will not see a single line in it. In this cultural center you will also get to see the classic car collection and in front of this center I Love Baku is written. People go here and take photos or videos. The ticket price of Heydar Aliyev Cultural Center ticket price is 15 AZN in Indian currency INR 772. Number 11 Yanar Dag If you go to visit Baku, then you must visit Yanar Dag because this is the biggest identity of Azerbaijan. Natural gases are found here, due to this there is a fire here for 4000 years which never gets extinguished. The special thing here is that even if it rains or storms come here, the fire here does not blowout. The fire in some areas has been extinguished but what is special here. The place is in Yanar Dag, here the fire never blowout, it always burns. The ticket price of Yanar Dag is 10 AZN. No.12 Ateshgah Temple Baku. If you are going to visit Baku, then you must visit the Temple Ateshgah because here you will get to see the history. Earlier Persian people used to live here. Atash means fire which is a Persian word. In this temple it is said that earlier fire used to burn naturally. But still the fire burns but it does not burn naturally but it always burns. The idol of Lord Ganesha is kept in this temple and Om Ganeshaya Namah is always recited by music. Some words are written in Sanskrit and Punjabi in the temple. Earlier the Silk Road used to go through Baku, so earlier the Indians who used to go for trade used to stay there, that is why the idol of Lord Ganesha is kept there. Indians used to worship there and Guru Gobind Singh Ji also visited here. No. 13 Gobustan Rock Art Cultural Landscape. This is a UNESCO heritage site. There used to be a human civilization in Gobustan, the proof of which can still be seen. There are many types of carvings on the stones, such as human figures, animals, plants, fishing scenes. Apart from rock art, remains of burial settlements have also been found here. There is a special stone placed at the entrance of the Gobustan Rock Cart, when it is played, So you will get to hear a different sound. Its ticket price is 10 AZN . No. 14 Mud Volcanoes. This is at a distance of about 20 kilometers from Nizam Street. Azerbaijan has the highest number of volcanoes in the world. About 50 volcanoes in the world are in Azerbaijan only. Here, methane gas comes out due to which volcanoes are formed. Lava does not come out in the volcanoes here, mud comes out, soil comes out, hence mud. It is called Volcano, it is also called Pink Volcano. If you set fire to Mud Volcano, fire also starts. There is no entry fee for Mud Volcanoes, but from there you will have to go by taxi. There are taxis which take 15 AZN for one side and 30 AZN for two side. You can get to see the taxis of the times of USSR, so if you are going by private taxi or by any tourism company. If you are staying then you will have to stop your car. To go to Mud Volcano, you will have to take this taxi. No. 15 Bibi haibat Mosque, Bibiheybat Mosque is one of the famous mosque of Baku. This beautiful mosque was built in the 13th century but it was destroyed in 1934. It was rebuilt again in 1990. This mosque is built on the banks of the Caspian Sea and its architecture looks very beautiful from inside. So friends, I hope you liked this informative video. Where are you watching the video from? Do tell by commenting. If you get any help from my video, I will be very happy. So take care of yourself, take care of your family. See you in the next video. Jai Hind. [Music]
